canonical_runtime_note: This mirrors the PROPOSED (non-canonical) strong_wind_solar v2.0 artifact in Damage_Modeling/docs/cells/strong_wind_solar/proposed/. canonical_runtime_artifact=FALSE and promotion=not_performed upstream. It is a PREVIEW only and MUST NOT be used as the runtime crosswalk. The canonical crosswalk remains the v1.0 file (value_basis_from_damage_modeling.json).
important_rule: PROPOSED preview. Normalization/grain crosswalk only; NO pass/fail bands, NO curve tuning. The lower/central/upper state medians are an EPISTEMIC ENVELOPE (screening uncertainty), NOT statistical percentiles of a loss distribution.
scope_note: v2.0 keeps a single straight_line_convective hazard input but ROUTES it by array_architecture into two SCREENING pathways: (1) fixed_tilt_ground_mount_screening_v1 and (2) single_axis_tracker_qualified_screening_v1. Tornado and hurricane/TC remain fenced out. Grade upstream: SCREENING_ENGINEERING_PROXY.

fixed_tilt_ground_mount_screening_v1.x_axis: x_fixed = event net-pressure demand / qualified design net-pressure

single_axis_tracker_qualified_screening_.x_axis: x_tracker = tracker-normal 3s gust / exact-system Ucrit (critical galloping/stow speed)
single_axis_tracker_qualified_screening_.demand_proxy: tracker-normal gust vs system-specific Ucrit (CPP 2015 galloping mechanism)

strong_wind_damage_grain_v2.f_kind: engineering demand-vs-capacity proxy per architecture (NOT geometry, NOT BOM material share)
strong_wind_damage_grain_v2.owns: severity screening states only — EAL/PML/VaR/TVaR downstream

state_cost_ratios.note: ordered damage states carry cost_ratio + capacity_scenarios (lower/central/upper state_medians = epistemic envelope, NOT percentiles). Exact ordinates live in the upstream proposed artifact; this preview uses illustrative central/lower/upper medians for the reference table.

comparability_caveat: fixed_tilt and single_axis_tracker use DIFFERENT demand denominators (net-pressure ratio vs gust/Ucrit) and are NOT on a common x-axis. Never regress one pathway against the other or against the v1.0 R_eff axis.
comparison_guidance: Same as v1.0: blended AXIS stow $/MW cannot be placed on a per-pathway state DR without architecture + gust + MW. A $/MW above a hardware cap is a grain warning, not DR>1.
